briefdescription.para: Handle an out of memory condition.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlOutputBufferPtr out, int code, xmlNodePtr node, const char *extra)
memberdef.name: xmlSaveErr

briefdescription.para: Handle an out of memory condition.
Sets the indent string.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if the string is NULL, empty or too long.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a saving context.
Free a saving context, destroying the output in any remaining buffer.
Create a new saving context.

simplesect.para: the new structure or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Serialize the attribute in the buffer.
This will dump the content the notation declaration as an XML DTD definition.
This is called with the hash scan function, and just reverses args.
This will dump the content of the notation table as an XML DTD definition.
Dump the occurrence operator of an element.
This will dump the content of the element table as an XML DTD definition.
This will dump the content of the element declaration as an XML DTD definition.
This will dump the content of the enumeration.
This will dump the content of the attribute declaration as an XML DTD definition.
This will dump the quoted string value, taking care of the special treatment required by %.
This will dump the content of the entity table as an XML DTD definition.
Dump an XHTML node, recursive behaviour, children are printed too.
Dump an XML node, recursive behaviour, children are printed too.
Dump an XML document.
Write out formatting for non-significant whitespace output.

parameterdescription.para: Number of extra indents to apply to ctxt->level

briefdescription.para: Dump a local Namespace definition.

detaileddescription.para: Should be called in the context of attributes dumps. If ctxt is supplied, buf should be its buffer.

briefdescription.para: Dump a list of local namespace definitions to a save context.

detaileddescription.para: Should be called in the context of attribute dumps.

briefdescription.para: Serialize a list of namespace definitions.
Dump the XML document DTD, if any.
Dump an XML attribute.
Dump an HTML node, recursive behaviour, children are printed too.
Check if a node is an empty xhtml node.

simplesect.para: 1 if the node is an empty node, 0 if not and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Dump a list of XML attributes.
Create a document saving context serializing to a file descriptor with the encoding and the options given.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is NULL, xmlSaveDoc uses the document's encoding and xmlSaveTree uses UTF-8.
This function doesn't allow to distinguish unsupported encoding errors from failed memory allocations.

simplesect.para: a new serialization context or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Create a document saving context serializing to a filename with the encoding and the options given.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is NULL, xmlSaveDoc uses the document's encoding and xmlSaveTree uses UTF-8.
This function doesn't allow to distinguish unsupported encoding errors from failed memory allocations.

parameterdescription.para: a file name or an URL
the encoding name to use or NULL

simplesect.para: a new serialization context or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Create a document saving context serializing to a buffer with the encoding and the options given.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is NULL, xmlSaveDoc uses the document's encoding and xmlSaveTree uses UTF-8.
This function doesn't allow to distinguish unsupported encoding errors from failed memory allocations.

parameterdescription.para: the encoding name to use or NULL

simplesect.para: a new serialization context or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlOutputWriteCallback iowrite, xmlOutputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlSaveToIO

briefdescription.para: Create a document saving context serializing to a file descriptor with the encoding and the options given.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is NULL, xmlSaveDoc uses the document's encoding and xmlSaveTree uses UTF-8.
This function doesn't allow to distinguish unsupported encoding errors from failed memory allocations.

parameterdescription.para: the encoding name to use or NULL

simplesect.para: a new serialization context or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Serialize a document.

detaileddescription.para: If the save context has no encoding, uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, uses ASCII without an encoding declaration.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Serialize a subtree starting.

detaileddescription.para: If the save context has no encoding, uses UTF-8.

parameterdescription.para: the root of the subtree to save

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Serialize a notation declaration.

simplesect.para: 0 on succes, -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Serialize notation declarations of a document.

simplesect.para: 0 on succes, -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Flush a document saving context, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: make sure that all buffered input has been processed.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Close a document saving context, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: make sure that all buffered input has been processed and free the context struct.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Close a document saving context, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: make sure that all buffered input has been processed and free the context struct.

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

briefdescription.para: Set a custom escaping function to be used for text in element content.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Has no effect.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Serialize attribute text to an output buffer.
Serialize attribute text to an xmlBuffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlBuffer *buf, xmlDoc *doc, xmlNode *cur, int level, int format)
memberdef.name: xmlNodeDump

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML node to an xmlBuffer.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, uses ASCII without an encoding declaration.
Note that format only works if the document was parsed with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.
Since this is using xmlBuffer structures it is limited to 2GB and somewhat deprecated, use xmlNodeDumpOutput instead.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written to the buffer or -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlBuf *buf, xmlDoc *doc, xmlNode *cur, int level, int format)
memberdef.name: xmlBufNodeDump

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML node to an xmlBuf.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, uses ASCII without an encoding declaration.
Note that format only works if the document was parsed with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written to the buffer, in case of error 0 is returned or buf stores the error

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML node to a FILE.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, uses ASCII without an encoding declaration.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlOutputBuffer *buf, xmlDoc *doc, xmlNode *cur, int level, int format, const char *encoding)
memberdef.name: xmlNodeDumpOutput

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML node to an output buffer.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is NULL, uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, serializes as ASCII without an encoding declaration.
Note that format only works if the document was parsed with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlOutputBufferPtr buf, xmlDocPtr doc, const char *encoding, int format)
memberdef.name: xmlDocDumpInternal

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *out_doc, xmlChar **doc_txt_ptr, int *doc_txt_len, const char *txt_encoding, int format)
memberdef.name: xmlDocDumpFormatMemoryEnc

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML document to memory.

detaileddescription.para: If encoding is NULL, uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, serializes as ASCII without an encoding declaration.
It is up to the caller of this function to free the returned memory with xmlFree.
Note that format only works if the document was parsed with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.

parameterdescription.para: Document to generate XML text from
Memory pointer for allocated XML text
Length of the generated XML text
Character encoding to use when generating XML text

briefdescription.para: Same as xmlDocDumpFormatMemoryEnc with encoding set to NULL and format set to 0.
Same as xmlDocDumpFormatMemoryEnc with encoding set to NULL.
Same as xmlDocDumpFormatMemoryEnc with format set to 0.

parameterdescription.para: Document to generate XML text from
Memory pointer for allocated XML text
Length of the generated XML text
Character encoding to use when generating XML text

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML document to a FILE.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, uses ASCII without an encoding declaration.
Note that format only works if the document was parsed with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML document to a FILE.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, uses ASCII without an encoding declaration.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Same as xmlSaveFormatFileTo with format set to 0.

detaileddescription.para: WARNING: This calls xmlOutputBufferClose and frees buf.

parameterdescription.para: the encoding if any assuming the I/O layer handles the transcoding

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML document to an output buffer.

detaileddescription.para: If the output buffer already uses a (non-default) encoding, encoding is ignored. If the output buffer has no encoding and encoding is NULL, uses the document's encoding or ASCII without an encoding declaration.
Note that format only works if the document was parsed with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.
WARNING: This calls xmlOutputBufferClose and frees buf.

parameterdescription.para: the encoding if any assuming the I/O layer handles the transcoding

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *filename, xmlDoc *cur, const char *encoding, int format)
memberdef.name: xmlSaveFormatFileEnc

briefdescription.para: Serialize an XML document to a file using the given encoding.

detaileddescription.para: If filename is "-", stdout is used. This is potentially insecure and might be changed in a future version.
If encoding is NULL, uses the document's encoding. If the document has no encoding, serializes as ASCII without an encoding declaration.
Note that format only works if the document was parsed with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.

parameterdescription.para: the filename or URL to output
the name of the encoding to use or NULL.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Same as xmlSaveFormatFileEnc with format set to 0.

parameterdescription.para: the name of an encoding (or NULL)

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Same as xmlSaveFormatFileEnc with encoding set to NULL.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: Same as xmlSaveFormatFileEnc with encoding set to NULL and format set to 0.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or -1 in case of failure.
